checks.finding: All in-scope requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-011) map to at least one AC with concrete, checkable outcomes and a listed test target in acceptance-tests.md. AC-005's aggregation rule is now fully quantified (PASS requires both reviewers free of Critical/Major FAIL; NEEDS_WORK before round 3; BLOCKED at round 3; round-3 Minor-only yields PASS with nonzero warningCount), resolving the round-1 ambiguity. AC-016 (update-mode: edited stage plus downstream stages re-run in confirmation mode, upstream byte-identical, status reset to Pending) and AC-017 (panelist-unavailable recorded, requires_human_decision set, no auto-continue) are both concretely observable via named test files (TEST-016, TEST-017).

Each of the four Goals traces to at least one AC: opt-in public entry point -> AC-001; independent two-reviewer review plus cross-model verification -> AC-005, AC-006, AC-017; deterministic downstream conformance -> AC-008, AC-009, AC-015; zero impact when domain/ absent -> AC-010. No orphaned goal found.

All 17 ACs describe externally observable outcomes: specific file paths produced (AC-002), schema validation result (AC-003), field values and status transitions (AC-007, AC-014, AC-016), report contents and gate verdicts (AC-005, AC-006, AC-009, AC-017), and pass/warn conditions tied to fixtures in acceptance-tests.md (AC-015 two-context fixtures). No AC states only intent without an observable artifact or state change.

Non-goals section explicitly excludes language-specific code generation templates, CQRS/Event Sourcing support, lite-track integration, and C4 Component/Code levels (Container level only). Given the seven-stage pipeline and reverse-generation are pulled in-scope per the interview deltas noted in Overview, the corresponding Main Workflows (1-3) and Edge Cases define their boundaries (e.g., reverse mode only seeds an interview, still requires the same review path; update mode leaves upstream stages untouched).

Security Boundaries table specifies auth/authz, data classification, and regulatory constraints for the three trust boundaries (seed inputs, Domain-Model-Status guard, domain-contract.json consumption). R-10 protected-file constraint is called out in Risks (R-1) with the scratchpad-to-human-copy procedure. Enforcement-level constraint (warn-by-default, SDD_DOMAIN_ENFORCE=error to escalate) is explicit in AC-009. Version-lock constraint across all seven plugins is stated in Assumptions and AC-011.

Each listed risk has a validation surface: R-1 (guard-script edits touching protected files) is validated via AC-007/AC-014 hook-guard tests; R-2 (cross-model verification cost) mitigation of running once per review-loop PASS is consistent with AC-006's placement before the approval gate; R-3 (over-eager conformance blocking specs) is validated by AC-009's warn-first default and AC-015's precise declared-relation matching test; R-4 (interview fatigue) mitigation of stage checkpointing is consistent with AC-016's update-mode re-run semantics leaving upstream stages byte-identical.
